req.title: Hr-Admin updates leave-request
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a leave-request through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-014 requires an authenticated hr-admin to update leave-requests via UI form with mandatory field validation, audit trail generation, and proper HTTP status codes. The test suite covers: (1) positive case with valid mandatory fields and audit verification; (2) negative case with missing mandatory field and 400 response; (3) negative case with insufficient role authorization; (4) boundary case with maximum-length field values; (5) state transition from pending to approved state.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_valid_mandatory_fields
preconditions: leave-request with ID lr-001 exists in pending state
actions: query audit log for leave-request update event
expected: HTTP 200 response received
response body contains updated leave-request entity with ID lr-001
audit record created with event_type=leave_request_update, actor=hr-admin, timestamp within 5 seconds
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_valid_mandatory_fields(
    auth_token, base_url, audit_client
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    payload = {
        "status": "approved",
        "reason": "Manager approval"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/leave-requests/lr-001",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["id"] == "lr-001"
    assert data["status"] == "approved"
    
    audit_logs = audit_client.query(
        event_type="leave_request_update",
        entity_id="lr-001"
    )
    assert len(audit_logs) > 0
    latest = audit_logs[-1]
    assert latest["actor"] == "hr-admin"
    assert (
        datetime.fromisoformat(latest["timestamp"]) >
        datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(seconds=5)
    )

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_missing_mandatory_field
actions: verify response status code and error message
expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request response
error response contains message indicating missing mandatory field 'status'
no audit record created for this failed request
trace: R-WEB-014: error pathway 400 on validation error

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_insufficient_role
preconditions: authenticated session with employee role (not hr-admin)
actions: POST /leave-requests/lr-003 with valid payload using employee token
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden response
response body contains message indicating role is not authorized
no leave-request update occurs
trace: R-WEB-014: error pathway 403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_unauthenticated
actions: POST /leave-requests/lr-004 with valid payload and no auth header
expected: HTTP 401 Unauthorized response
response body contains authentication error message
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_unauthenticated(base_url):
    payload = {
        "status": "approved",
        "reason": "test"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/leave-requests/lr-004",
        json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    error = resp.json()
    assert "unauthorized" in error["message"].lower() or \
           "authenticate" in error["message"].lower()

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_max_length_reason_field
preconditions: reason field has maximum length constraint of 500 characters
actions: POST /leave-requests/lr-005 with reason field set to exactly 500 characters
verify response status and entity content
expected: HTTP 200 response received
leave-request updated with full reason text
reason field in response contains all 500 characters
trace: R-WEB-014: boundary value analysis on reason field length
R-WEB-014: maximum valid length processed successfully

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_exceeds_max_length_reason
preconditions: reason field has maximum length constraint of 500 characters
actions: POST /leave-requests/lr-006 with reason field set to 501 characters
expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request response
error message indicates field length violation
trace: R-WEB-014: boundary value analysis beyond maximum

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_state_transition_pending_to_approved
preconditions: leave-request with ID lr-007 exists in pending state
actions: GET /leave-requests/lr-007 to verify current state is pending
GET /leave-requests/lr-007 to verify new state is approved
expected: initial GET returns status pending
POST update returns HTTP 200
final GET returns status approved
state transition recorded in audit log
trace: R-WEB-014: leave-request updated to new state
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_state_transition_pending_to_approved(
    auth_token, base_url, audit_client
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    
    get1 = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/leave-requests/lr-007",
        headers=headers
    )
    assert get1.json()["status"] == "pending"
    
    update = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/leave-requests/lr-007",
        json={"status": "approved", "reason": "test"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert update.status_code == 200
    
    get2 = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/leave-requests/lr-007",
        headers=headers
    )
    assert get2.json()["status"] == "approved"
    
    logs = audit_client.query(
        event_type="leave_request_update",
        entity_id="lr-007"
    )
    assert any(
        "approved" in log.get("details", "").lower()
        for log in logs
    )
